title: campanula subalpina
intro: Campanula subalpina, also known as the Alpine Campanula, is a perennial flowering plant native to the mountainous regions of Europe and Asia. It has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which are attributed to the presence of saponins and flavonoids in its leaves and flowers. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving pain and inflammation, and supporting the immune system. In herbal preparations, Campanula subalpina is often used to make teas, tinctures, and infusions, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety for human use.
